machine_spec.turbo: no_turbo=1 (attempted; sudo unavailable for cpupower during run)

note: All numbers from Bitcoin Core bench_bitcoin binary — real Bitcoin Core validation pipeline. NOT bench_unified. bench_bitcoin internal harness reports median with err% per operation. No external CPU pinning beyond taskset; governor=performance. Signing speedups (14-36%) are large enough to be conclusive despite no hard turbo lock.

VerifyScriptP2WPKH_ns.verdict: Ultra +1.9% (within noise margin — treat as parity)

VerifyScriptP2TR_KeyPath_ns.verdict: Ultra +3.0% (marginal; within noise at high err%)

BIP324_ECDH_ns.note: From 2026-05-08 run; not re-measured in 2026-05-11 run. Within noise margin.

results_nolto.note: Release without LTO. Static w=8 table + branchless wNAF sign + selective MULX+ADCX/ADOX ASM in verify-only Jacobian ops. Numbers from 2026-05-08 run; not re-measured 2026-05-11.
results_nolto.build: SECP256K1_BUILD_SHIM=ON + SECP256K1_CORE_BACKEND_MODE=ON. fe52_mul_inner_var + jac52_*_var route verify hot path through dual-carry ASM; CT signing untouched.

test_suite.note: 749/749 Bitcoin Core unit tests pass with Ultra backend

nolto_gap_root_cause.finding: ConnectBlock -1.1% without LTO is caused by ultra's larger instruction footprint, NOT by secp256k1 operation latency.
evidence: VerifyScriptP2WPKH (full verify path): ultra 45.5µs ≈ libsecp 45.1µs → secp256k1 ops at parity
VerifyScriptP2TR_KeyPath: ultra 44.5µs vs libsecp 45.7µs → ultra 1.03x FASTER
VerifyScriptP2TR_ScriptPath: ultra 64.6µs vs libsecp 83.0µs → ultra 1.28x FASTER
ultra .text section: 10.5 MB vs libsecp .text: 9.6 MB (difference: ~900 KB)
With LTO: ConnectBlock ratio = 1.01-1.02 (global layout optimization eliminates cache pressure)
nolto_gap_root_cause.root_cause: Ultra's more sophisticated algorithms produce ~1.3 MB of secp256k1 code vs libsecp's ~400 KB. Without LTO, extra 900 KB competes for L2/L3 instruction cache. LTO eliminates the gap by co-optimizing code layout globally.
nolto_gap_root_cause.recommendation: Use LTO for production builds (-DCMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION=ON). ConnectBlock parity/win confirmed with LTO. For non-LTO builds, the ~1% gap is a known trade-off: signing 14-36% faster, verify paths equal or faster.

known_limitations: ConnectBlock 1% gap without LTO: residual after 3 optimizations. LTO eliminates it (ratio 1.01-1.02 LTO, 0.989 no-LTO).
BIP324 ECDH: 2% within noise margin. No optimized CT ECDH path yet.
No hard CPU turbo lock during 2026-05-11 benchmark run (sudo unavailable). err% is 0.1-1.3% indicating stable results.
